todos.content: [REQ] Phase 1 — Context: module name, purpose, SoC position
todos.activeForm: [REQ] Phase 1 — Context: module name, purpose, SoC position
todos.detail:
Ask the user:
- [ ] Module name (this becomes <ip_name>)
- [ ] One-sentence purpose: what does this IP do?
- [ ] SoC context: where does it sit? (peripheral bus, subsystem, standalone)
- [ ] Technology / process node (if relevant)
Create folder: <ip_name>/req/
Gate: IP name confirmed by user.

todos.content: [REQ] Phase 2 — Interface: clocks, reset, ports, parameters
todos.activeForm: [REQ] Phase 2 — Interface: clocks, reset, ports, parameters
todos.detail:
Ask the user about each item. Confirm before moving on.
- [ ] Clock domains: name and frequency for each
- [ ] Reset: synchronous or asynchronous? Active-high or active-low?
- [ ] Top-level ports: for each port ask Name / Width / Direction / Clock Domain / Description. Ask port by port if the user has no list.
- [ ] Parameters/generics: name, default value, description
Gate: All ports have explicit width and direction. No 'TBD' port widths.

todos.content: [REQ] Phase 3 — Functional requirements: features, FSM, latency
todos.activeForm: [REQ] Phase 3 — Functional requirements: features, FSM, latency
todos.detail:
Ask the user about each item one at a time. Confirm before moving on.
- [ ] List all major features/modes (enumerate them — ask 'any more?' after each)
- [ ] For each feature: trigger condition, data flow, expected output
- [ ] Main FSM states (high level): state name, what triggers entry, what happens
- [ ] Latency requirement: max cycles from input to output
- [ ] Throughput: transactions per cycle at full load
Gate: At least one feature fully described with trigger and output.

todos.content: [REQ] Phase 4 — Register map: interface type, offsets, bitfields
todos.activeForm: [REQ] Phase 4 — Register map: interface type, offsets, bitfields
todos.detail:
Ask the user:
- [ ] Register interface type: APB / AXI-Lite / custom / none
- [ ] Base address (if known)
- [ ] For each register: Offset / Name / Width / Access (RW/RO/WO/W1C) / Reset value / Description
- [ ] For registers with bitfields: Bits / Name / Access / Description
- [ ] Mark reserved bits explicitly
If no registers: write 'Register Map: N/A'
Gate: Every register has a unique offset and access type.

Ask the user:
- [ ] Does this IP generate interrupts? (yes/no)
- [ ] If yes, for each source: name, trigger condition, bit position in status register, clear mechanism (W1C / auto-clear / sw-clear)
- [ ] irq output signal: name, polarity, type (level/edge)
- [ ] Enable/mask mechanism
If no interrupts: write 'Interrupt: N/A'
Gate: Each interrupt source has a unique bit and a defined clear path.

Ask the user:
- [ ] Does this IP have internal memory? (RAMs, FIFOs, register files) (yes/no)
- [ ] If yes, for each instance: type (SRAM/FIFO/RF), depth, width, number of read ports, number of write ports, latency in cycles
- [ ] Initialization state at reset
- [ ] ECC or parity requirements
If no memory: write 'Memory: N/A'
Gate: Each memory instance has defined depth and latency.

Ask the user:
- [ ] Target clock frequency (MHz)
- [ ] Maximum input-to-output pipeline latency (cycles)
- [ ] Any CDC (clock domain crossing) paths? List crossing and synchronizer type.
- [ ] Critical path (if known)
- [ ] Any timing exceptions or multicycle paths
Gate: Clock frequency is a concrete number, not a range or 'TBD'.

todos.content: [REQ] Phase 8 — DV requirements: test scenarios, corner cases, SVA
todos.activeForm: [REQ] Phase 8 — DV requirements: test scenarios, corner cases, SVA
todos.detail:
Ask the user:
- [ ] List key test scenarios (minimum 3): scenario name + what it verifies
- [ ] Known corner cases or hazard conditions
- [ ] Required SVA assertions (if any)
- [ ] Coverage goals (functional coverage, code coverage targets)
- [ ] Any specific simulation tools or constraints
Gate: At least 3 test scenarios documented.

todos.content: [REQ] Phase 9 — Review with user and write requirements.md
todos.activeForm: [REQ] Phase 9 — Review with user and write requirements.md
todos.detail:
Present complete requirements summary to the user.
- [ ] Show all sections: Context, Interface, Features, Registers, Interrupt, Memory, Timing, DV
- [ ] List any Open Items (TBD or unresolved)
- [ ] Ask: 'Is this correct? Anything to add or change before I write the file?'
- [ ] Incorporate final user feedback
- [ ] Write <ip_name>/req/<ip_name>_requirements.md
- [ ] Output [REQ HANDOFF] → mas_gen block
Gate: User has explicitly approved the requirements. File written.
